Quick reference. Python project bootstrapped from ForgeWorks.
Update this file whenever the layout changes.

Living docs:
docs/structure.txt                 # this file
docs/PRODUCT_VISION.md             # north star -- what we're building and why (stable)
docs/requirements.md               # what we're building, for whom, why
docs/language-standards.md         # language- and tooling-specific conventions
docs/documentation.md              # links to library docs the agent should consult
docs/backlog.md                    # scoped, queued tasks
docs/proposals-ideas.md            # out-of-scope ideas, reviewed periodically
docs/gotchas.md                    # known pitfalls, anti-patterns, lessons (living)
docs/SECURITY.md                   # threat model, defenses, red-team checklist (living)
docs/current-task/task.md          # active task brief, shared between agents
docs/current-task/task-template.md # template; copy over task.md when starting a new task
docs/designs/                      # one design memo per non-trivial slice (the pre-code gate)
docs/probes/                       # recorded real observations of external collaborators
docs/ships/                        # one ship record per shipped slice (durable delivery evidence)

Source code (use your language's idiomatic layout; see AGENTS.md <architecture-discipline>
and docs/language-standards.md). A placeholder module + passing test ship so the gate is
green on first run -- replace them with your first slice. Two-layer split (backend / frontend
or app) when the project has a UI; a single layer otherwise.

Tests (unit + functional run in the fast gate; e2e is separate -- see docs/language-standards.md):
   placeholder smoke test ships green; e2e tests live apart (tests/e2e/ or build-tag gated)

Configuration:
pyproject.toml                  # dependencies + tool config + bundled scripts
.env.example                       # template for environment variables (never commit .env)
.gitignore
README.md
(some language profiles add more here -- e.g. a pre-commit config; see docs/language-standards.md)

Agent infrastructure:
.mcp.json                          # MCP server config (Context7 for live library docs)
AGENTS.md                          # the constitution (read first, every conversation)
.claude/hooks/slice-audit.sh       # agent-neutral; CI runs it as a plain script for every roster
.claude/agents/                    # subagents: test-spec-writer, implementer, code-reviewer, security-reviewer, tech-debt
.claude/hooks/quality-gate.sh      # deterministic static+test gate (code-reviewer Stop event)
.claude/hooks/deps-guard.sh        # supply-chain guard (PreToolUse): blocks unvetted dependency installs
.claude/settings.json              # wires the deps-guard PreToolUse hook
.claude/skills/select-agents/      # /select-agents skill: reconciles docs/agents.json when the roster changes
CLAUDE.md                          # symlink to AGENTS.md (for Claude Code compatibility)

CI:
.github/workflows/qa.yml           # fast quality gate + separate e2e job, on PRs and pushes to main
.github/pull_request_template.md   # short PR checklist

Dev environment:
.devcontainer/                     # dev container config (if yes = yes)
(the qa / fix / e2e runners are shell scripts under scripts/ OR package scripts in
pyproject.toml, per the language profile -- the commands are in docs/language-standards.md)
